seed_problem: seasonal weighted frequency assignment (assign one channel per tower to minimize weighted same-channel conflict)
mutation: composite: the objective is not the sum or max of per-season conflict, but the SUM OF THE TWO WORST of seven season-specific conflict totals, over a single year-round assignment (fixed frequency-assignment coupled with seasonal-conflict-weights and sum-of-worst-two-scoring)
theme: radio channels that endure summer foliage and winter clarity
title: Seasonal Spectrum Assignment
strategies: trivial: round-robin channel assignment ch_i = ((i-1) mod C) + 1, ignoring all weights (the checker's own baseline B)

naive greedy: order towers by total edge weight summed over ALL seven seasons and greedily assign each the channel minimizing incremental TOTAL weighted conflict -- this average-weighting proxy is blind to which two seasons actually end up binding, so it happily lets 'individually cheap-looking' hot-season edges collide while fighting to avoid 'individually expensive-looking' edges whose damage is actually spread thin across five non-binding seasons

union-conservative variant: the same failure mode reframed as trying to avoid every interfering pair regardless of season, burning channel diversity on conflicts that never co-occur in a single season

insight (strong): directly optimize the true sum-of-worst-two objective with incremental coordinate descent -- for each tower, evaluate the resulting top-two season sum (not a total-weight proxy) for every channel choice, always steering collisions away from whichever pair of seasons is currently binding and load-balancing the sacrificed conflict across the five non-binding seasons so none of them grows large enough to become binding either
